seoTitle: Add and Style Bullet Points in Webflow
metaDescription: Insert a proper unordered list in Webflow, customize the bullet icons, and style spacing so lists render cleanly across breakpoints.
intro: Pasting raw bullet symbols into a text block breaks semantic HTML and confuses screen readers. Webflow's native List element builds a proper unordered list instead, and this guide covers inserting it, customizing the bullet style, and keeping spacing consistent with your site's typography.

steps.heading: Open the Add Elements panel
steps.body: Click the Add Elements button to access Webflow's component library.

steps.heading: Drag in a List element
steps.body: Find the List component and drag it onto your canvas to create an unordered list block.

steps.heading: Add your list items
steps.body: Click into each list item to enter your text, and use the text shortcut keys to quickly generate additional bullet items.

steps.heading: Customize the bullet icon
steps.body: Swap the default bullet symbol for a custom brand image or checkmark graphic through the element's style settings.

steps.heading: Set spacing and apply a global class
steps.body: Configure padding and line-height in the Style Manager, then assign a global list class so it applies consistently across your blog posts.

faq.question: Why should I use the List element instead of pasting bullet symbols?
faq.answer: The List element produces proper semantic HTML, which reads correctly for screen readers and keeps formatting consistent.

faq.question: Can I change the bullet points to custom icons?
faq.answer: Yes, you can replace the default bullet with a custom uploaded image or checkmark graphic.

faq.question: How do I keep list styling consistent across my whole site?
faq.answer: Assign a global list class in the Style Manager so any new list automatically inherits the same typography and spacing.

faq.question: Does the list layout adjust for mobile screens?
faq.answer: Yes, test your list at different responsive breakpoints to confirm indentation and spacing hold up on smaller screens.
